name: sat_negative_dependency
description: Explaining a true literal derived through negation: a is true because rule 'a :- not c.' fired and c is false. Also a regression test for set_rules_for_order: with a single-letter atom 'd' the old substring matching hit the '%Add Answer Set' marker (d is inside 'Add'), silently dropping the whole answer-set section and losing the 'not c.' literal from the explanation.
program:
a :- not c.
c | d.
:- not d, not e.
